content.full_text:
RAy! MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
April 23,
1996
Sharon Jones
City of Pismo Beach
P.O.
Box
3
Pisxno Beach,
CA
93448
Re:
Combined Campaign Reports
Our File No.
1-96-135
Dear Ms. Jones:
This is to confirm telephone assistance provided to you
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act.
1
!
This letter constitutes written advice under
Section 84205,
which states:
The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit
candidates and committees to file campaign statements
combining statements and reports required to be filed by this
title.
A recall election has been called to be voted upon within the
City of Pismo Beach on July 9,
1996.
In addition,
a measure will
be placed on the same ballot.
Since the period for filing the
first semi—annual statement for 1996 covers only eight days before
the commencement of the second semi—annual filing period,
we
believe it is appropriate for committees primarily formed to
support or oppose the recall, the officeholder who is the subject
of the recall and candidates being voted upon in the recall
election, their controlled committees,
committees which are
primarily formed to support or oppose those candidates,
and
committees primarily formed to support or oppose the ballot
measure to file campaign disclosure statements on the following
schedule:
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Ms.
Jones
Our File No.
1-96-135
Page
2
Type of Statement
1st Pre-Election
2nd Pre-Election
Semi—Annual
Period Covered
Jan.
1
- May 25
May 26
- June
22
June 23
- Dec.
31
Filing Deadline
May 30
June 27
January 31,
1997
If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916)
322—5660.
Sincerely,
Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

sections.question_synthetic: May candidates and committees involved in a City of Pismo Beach recall election and ballot measure combine their campaign disclosure filing statements and reports, and if so, what filing schedule should be followed?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC permits committees formed to support or oppose the recall, the recalled officeholder, recall election candidates, and committees supporting the ballot measure to file combined campaign disclosure statements on a modified schedule with three filings: the period January 1 through May 25 (due May 30), May 26 through June 22 (due June 27), and June 23 through December 31 (due January 31, 1997).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers; document is structured as a letter confirming telephone advice and citing Section 84205 authority. Question and conclusion extracted from letter content and context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May candidates and committees involved in a City of Pismo Beach recall election and ballot measure combine their campaign disclosure filing statements and reports, and if so, what filing schedule should be followed?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC permits committees formed to support or oppose the recall, the recalled officeholder, recall election candidates, and committees supporting the ballot measure to file combined campaign disclosure statements on a modified schedule with three filings: the period January 1 through May 25 (due May 30), May 26 through June 22 (due June 27), and June 23 through December 31 (due January 31, 1997).
embedding.first_500_words: RAy! MEHTA CHAIRMAN FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION April 23, 1996 Sharon Jones City of Pismo Beach P.O. Box 3 Pisxno Beach, CA 93448 Re: Combined Campaign Reports Our File No. 1-96-135 Dear Ms. Jones: This is to confirm telephone assistance provided to you regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. 1 ! This letter constitutes written advice under Section 84205, which states: The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this title. A recall election has been called to be voted upon within the City of Pismo Beach on July 9, 1996. In addition, a measure will be placed on the same ballot. Since the period for filing the first semi—annual statement for 1996 covers only eight days before the commencement of the second semi—annual filing period, we believe it is appropriate for committees primarily formed to support or oppose the recall, the officeholder who is the subject of the recall and candidates being voted upon in the recall election, their controlled committees, committees which are primarily formed to support or oppose those candidates, and committees primarily formed to support or oppose the ballot measure to file campaign disclosure statements on the following schedule: 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, q. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660 Ms. Jones Our File No. 1-96-135 Page 2 Type of Statement 1st Pre-Election 2nd Pre-Election Semi—Annual Period Covered Jan. 1 - May 25 May 26 - June 22 June 23 - Dec. 31 Filing Deadline May 30 June 27 January 31, 1997 If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322—5660. Sincerely, Trish Mayer Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division
embedding.summary: The FPPC provides written advice confirming that entities involved in a July 9, 1996 recall election in Pismo Beach may file combined campaign disclosure statements on a specially tailored schedule to accommodate the unusual timing of the election.
